decision.summary: Issue #602 is complete as a reusable scheduling, aggregate, cancellation, and lifecycle boundary milestone. The exact admissions that opened are narrow aggregate slices with checked artifacts; executor, cancellation, scheduler, timer, interval, and cross-language lifecycle surfaces remain fail-closed as named obligations instead of API-specific expansion.
why_stop: The remaining surfaces require deeper dependency-closed models for callback identity/effects, task and microtask ordering, cancellation cleanup, interval cardinality, executor settlement ordering, and cross-language lifecycle protocols.
Continuing inside this epic would become API-by-API expansion instead of strengthening the semantic kernel's reusable capability vocabulary.
The current artifacts and tests preserve unsafe boundaries while making the next exact slices measurable.

artifact_coverage.evidence: 120-repo scan priced 142,844 scheduling/lifecycle-shaped occurrences and ranked Promise aggregates, executor timing, AbortSignal cancellation, interval lifecycle, Go goroutines, Java CompletableFuture, and Swift await.

Opened only fulfilled Promise.all over literal arrays whose elements already prove fulfilled Promise state; broad Promise.all, dynamic iterables, rejected elements, thenables, and sync array equivalence stayed closed.
Opened only Promise.allSettled literal arrays whose elements already prove fulfilled or rejected Promise state; ordered settled-record channels stay behind a fulfilled Promise boundary.
Opened raw non-thenable-safe input assimilation only for already-admitted literal Promise.all and Promise.allSettled aggregates.
Opened first-observed Promise.race and Promise.any literal aggregate recovery only for fully closed literal arrays; all-rejected Promise.any and dynamic/thenable paths stayed closed.

Priced 795 new Promise constructor occurrences by executor shape, settlement calls, timer/scheduler mentions, multi-settlement, throw-to-rejection, side effects, and thenable payload risk; exact constructor admission stayed closed.
Priced AbortSignal/AbortController cancellation and signal-option surfaces; exact cancellation admission stayed closed.

Priced timer, scheduler, interval, and microtask surfaces, including 780 setTimeout calls, 73 bare setInterval calls, 133 clearTimeout calls, and 11 scheduler.yield calls; exact scheduling/lifecycle admission stayed closed.

capabilities_landed: Reusable aggregate-result-channel contract family for all-fulfilled, all-settled, first-settled, and first-fulfilled Promise aggregates.
Dependency-backed static-global Promise aggregate evidence without selector-only admission.
Non-thenable-safe raw input assimilation reused across Promise.resolve and admitted literal aggregate elements.
Named executor callback/timing/throw-to-rejection obligations for Promise-like constructor surfaces.
Named cancellation/liveness obligations for AbortSignal, AbortController, scheduler.wait, timers, animation frames, and intervals.
Named scheduling and lifecycle obligations for timer scheduling, scheduler.yield ordering, interval repeated emission, and cross-language lifecycle protocol candidates.
Hard-negative inventory that keeps sync/async erasure, thenable assimilation, all-vs-first settlement, cancellation, and interval liveness boundaries closed.
closed_boundaries_to_keep_closed_after_i: Untyped or object/function thenable assimilation without explicit non-thenable proof.
new Promise executor timing, multi-settlement precedence, and throw-to-rejection exact recovery.
AbortSignal cancellation ordering, abort reason propagation, signal-aware listener/fetch/timer cleanup, and controller-signal lifecycle exact recovery.
scheduler.wait/yield ordering, queueMicrotask, requestAnimationFrame, and timer/frame exact equivalence.
setInterval cardinality, repeated-emission lifecycle, and cleanup exact recovery.
Go goroutine/channel/defer/select, Java Future/Stream, Swift async/throws, Python async/iterator lifecycle, and Rust Future-like exact convergence.

next_work_policy.recommended_next_scope: Pick one closed boundary with current corpus prevalence and hard negatives, then open a narrow exact slice only when callback/effect/order/lifecycle proof is dependency-closed.
recommended_first_candidates: Promise executor settlement-order reporting to exact proof, if non-thenable payload and callback-effect obligations can be closed.
AbortSignal cancellation exact slice for a tiny signal identity and already-aborted path, if abort ordering and rejection cleanup are modeled.
Timer/scheduler exact slice only after callback identity/effect and ordering proofs are available.
